title: Firkea venosa
taxonomy: Firkea venosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. Firkea venosa is native to Australia and is often found growing on the branches of native trees. The classification of Firkea venosa can be summarized as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Santalales, family Loranthaceae, genus Firkea, species F. venosa.
